edition.dagbeeld: De Navo-top in Turkije domineert de agenda: Trump dreigt, Zelensky eist lidmaatschap, en in de Straat van Hormuz worden tankers beschoten. Tegelijk valt in Londen het laatste doek in de strijd van prins Harry tegen de tabloidpers. Veiligheid, geopolitieke spanning en mediamacht zijn de rode draden van deze editie.

article.title: Qatar says Iran fully responsible after tankers struck in strait of Hormuz
article.summary: Three vessels, including a Qatari LNG carrier, struck within hours close to Oman

opening.note: Tankers aangevallen bij Hormuz; Qatar wijst Iran aan als verantwoordelijke — escalatie dreigt.
opening.label: breaking

article.title: Trump renews call for US to take over Greenland as he arrives for Nato summit
article.summary: President also threatens to pull all American troops from Europe and rails against Nato stance on Iran war

front.note: Trump dreigt alle troepen uit Europa te trekken bij opening Navo-top.
front.label: breaking

article.title: Zelenskyy says Nato should let Ukraine join to ‘make all of us stronger’
article.summary: Ukraine’s president says defensive capabilities built up in war with Russia mean it would be wrong to exclude it

front.note: Zelensky claimt dat oorlogservaring Oekraïne tot sterke Navo-bondgenoot maakt.
front.label: analyse

article.title: Prince Harry could face £50m legal bill after losing phone-hacking lawsuit against Mail publisher
article.summary: Duke of Sussex and other prominent figures lost their case over claims the newspaper used unlawful methods to source stories about them

front.note: Harry verliest op alle fronten; rekening kan oplopen tot vijftig miljoen pond.
front.label: meest gelezen

article.title: Marine Le Pen says she will run for president and appeal conviction again
article.summary: An appeals court on Tuesday found the far-right leader guilty of embezzlement but shortened her ban on holding elected office, allowing her to stand in the 2027 election. However, it also sentenced her to at-home detention with an electronic tag for a year, although that term could be reduced.

front.note: Le Pen schuldig bevonden maar mag toch presidentsrace in, met enkelband.
front.label: breaking

article.title: Disney accuses US media regulator of trying ‘to sit in the editor’s chair’
article.summary: Media giant claims FCC investigation into talk show ‘The View’ could violate First Amendment of American constitution

front.note: Disney verzet zich: FCC-onderzoek naar 'The View' bedreigt persvrijheid.
front.label: analyse

article.title: U.S. gave Tehran details on Iranian asylum seekers, lawsuit alleges
article.summary: The claim, outlined in court papers, contends information shared with Iran could jeopardize the lives of pro-democracy protesters, religious minorities and LGTBQ people.

front.note: VS deelde identiteitsgegevens van Iraanse asielzoekers met Teheran, zegt rechtszaak.
front.label: onderzoek

article.title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
article.summary: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.

front.note: DeepSeek wil eigen chips bouwen om Amerikaanse exportcontroles te omzeilen.
front.label: analyse

items.title: Saudi Arabia blocking payments to UAE accounts, businesses say
items.summary: Transfers from kingdom to companies and individuals in Dubai repeatedly returned or delayed in a sign of economic tension

items.title: America Was Being Played. The Bessent Doctrine Says Those Days Are Over.
items.summary: The United States is changing the rules of trade in its favor.

items.title: Lawyer says detained Gaza doctor was severely beaten in Israeli jail
items.summary: The lawyer for Dr Hussam Abu Safiya tells the BBC his client was so badly beaten he could not recognise him during a visit last week.

items.title: Britain plans to build autonomous AI 'Cyber Shield' to defend nation
items.summary: The capability, called Cyber Shield, is designed to counter a threat the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) said could see attackers “move at machine speed and greater scale, reducing opportunities for detection and response.”

items.title: Grootste Russische raffinaderij stilgelegd na Oekraïense aanval, groeiende brandstoftekorten in het land
Gathering Clouds: Building Digital Strategic Depth in the Compute Age
items.summary: The wars in Ukraine and the Middle East have exposed a strategic reality that military planners are only beginning to confront: In a data-centric age, digital infrastructure has become part of the battlespace. Data centers and cloud regions are now the digital backbone of military power and economic prosperity. As such

items.title: China’s Answer to AI Sticker Shock
items.summary: Corporate America is starting to balk at the cost of AI agents. A cheap alternative from China looks more tempting than ever.

items.title: Cloudflare puts prices on AI scraping
items.summary: Cloudflare recently rolled out what is essentially a bot paywall that would allow its customers to charge AI models for scraping its customers’ content.

items.title: UK’s bet on AI will endure under Burnham, says minister
items.summary: Kanishka Narayan says he's met with Britain's likely next prime minister “to talk about the centrality” of AI for the U.K.

items.title: Cops Say Waymo Snitched on Teens for Allegedly Drinking and Shooting a Toy Gun
items.summary: The San Mateo Police Department posted on Facebook: “Parents do you know where your teens are? Waymo does!"

items.title: Supreme Court allows Texas app law requiring age verification to take effect
items.summary: A student advocacy organization and tech trade group had appealed to the high court to stay the Texas App Store Accountability Act on an emergency basis until the lower court rules.

items.title: Mysterious Compound Detected on Pluto and Titan
items.summary: Something on Pluto and one of Saturn’s moons, Titan, absorbs light in a way unexplained by anything in spectroscopic databases.

items.title: Shut Those Laptops! Anthropic Puts Its Claude Cowork Agent on Your Phone
items.summary: Claude Cowork now keeps working on tasks even after you close your laptop. It’s part of a larger push toward smartphone-controlled agents.

items.title: ‘Serious consequences’: Farage attacks Sky News after question about George Cottrell
items.summary: Reform UK leader confronts journalist after question about gifts from friend and convicted fraudster

items.title: Kristen Schaal: Letting kids on social media is like giving them cigarettes

items.summary: Articles dating from 1997 to 2015 formed the basis for 97 allegations of unlawful newsgathering. The post The 53 Mail articles vindicated by Prince Harry privacy judgment appeared first on Press Gazette .

items.title: Paul Dacre: Prince Harry privacy case ‘conspiracy to destroy paper’
items.summary: Case was funded by 'orgy-loving racist' Max Mosley and actor Hugh Grant, says Dacre. The post Paul Dacre: Prince Harry privacy case ‘conspiracy to destroy paper’ appeared first on Press Gazette .

items.title: Who is David Sherborne, the lawyer who persuaded Prince Harry to sue?
Lab-Leak Payback Has Begun
items.summary: Indictments, subpoenas, and debarments are hitting American scientists embroiled in the controversy over COVID’s origins.

items.title: Supreme Court justices to make rare appearance before Congress
items.summary: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett, a liberal and a conservative, are set to testify before a House subcommittee next Tuesday.

items.title: Hoe de overheid postcovid­patiënten ‘in de steek laat’ en daarmee zorgcowboys in de kaart speelt
items.summary: Het kabinet dreigt de financiering voor onderzoek naar postcovid stop te zetten. En dat terwijl er honderdduizenden patiënten nog steeds zonder behandeling zitten. Uit wanhoop experimenteren patiënten met schijnbehandelingen.

items.title: How a Four-Generation Cuban Family Survives on $60 a Month
items.summary: The U.S. oil blockade has deepened a humanitarian crisis, forcing Cubans to lean on the island’s long tradition of community solidarity to provide a cushion.

items.title: JD Vance Is Worried
items.summary: The vice president’s theology comes in book form.

items.title: George E. Johnson, Who Built a Black Hair-Care Empire, Dies at 99
items.summary: Mr. Johnson’s hair product company, a longtime sponsor of “Soul Train,” was the first Black-owned company listed on a major American stock exchange.

items.title: Labour, Tories and Lib Dems refuse to stand in byelection with Farage’s resignation labelled ‘desperate stunt’ – UK politics live
items.summary: The Reform UK leader will speak after Labour asked the Electoral Commission to investigate claims that he broke electoral law by not disclosing gifts

items.title: Argentina 3-2 Egypt: World Cup 2026 last 16 – live
items.summary: Minute-by-minute report: Lionel Messi’s Argentina stage remarkable fightback with three late goals to reach quarter-finals. Scott Murray has the reaction

items.title: Van ouderen verlangen dat ze in hun wooncomplexen naar elkaar omzien? Een boodschap meenemen, prima. Hulp bij steunkousen – dat gaat te ver
items.summary: Het kabinet kondigde maandag aan 420 miljoen euro extra te steken in de bouw van seniorenwoningen. Onder meer in woongemeenschappen voor ouderen die, als het even kan, elkaar de helpende hand bieden. Onderzoekers namen vier van zulke plekken onder de loep. Ouderen blijken te schipperen tussen verbondenheid en vrijheid

items.title: Wat de affaire-Balogun leert over het topvoetbal: een rode lijn bestaat niet, de lelijke kant wordt snel geaccepteerd
Miljardendeals aangekondigd op de NAVO-top in Turkije. De wapenfabrikanten glunderen van oor tot oor
